input:
Morning:
- Weekly huddle meeting
- Team huddle for Project 2 - discussed and delegated tasks
Afternoon:
- Tested workflow - monitored agent responses to different emails
- Flagged notable responses to report back to team
- Daily check-in with David and Matt for project updates
- Developed FastAPI-based replacement for CloudConvert service (PDF to images)
- Deployed it to Azure for use in n8n workflow
output: The day started with the weekly huddle followed by a team huddle for Project 2 where we discussed and delegated tasks. From there, I tested the workflow by monitoring agent responses to different emails and flagging notable ones to report back to the team. We also had our daily check-in with David and Matt to stay aligned on project updates. Later, I developed a FastAPI-based replacement for the CloudConvert PDF-to-image service and deployed it to Azure for use in the n8n workflow.

input:
Morning:
- Tried to automate the testing process - repetitive and time-consuming
- Some configs need editing directly in n8n, making full automation hard
- Connected Claude, Claude Code, and MCP into n8n workflow
Afternoon:
- Couldn't get automation working after a few hours
- Shifted back to manual testing
- Results mostly okay but some responses need improvement
- Tested different scenarios and flagged areas for improvement
output: I spent the morning trying to automate the testing process since it's repetitive and time-consuming, but some configurations need to be edited directly inside n8n, making full automation challenging. I also connected Claude, Claude Code, and MCP into the n8n workflow. After a few hours without getting the automation working, I shifted back to manual testing. The results were mostly okay, though I flagged several scenarios where the agent's responses still need improvement.

input:
Morning:
- Group huddle - went over yesterday's progress, delegated tasks
- Assigned to test workflow: bookings, connote lookups, quotation requests
Afternoon:
- Agent handled all test cases well
- Made adjustments to make workflow more stable
- Christian provided Claude plan
- Explored Claude capabilities for our workflow
Evening:
- Testing and logging agent responses
- Compiled everything into Excel sheet for tracking
output: We kicked off the morning with a group huddle to go over yesterday's progress and delegate tasks. I was assigned to test the workflow on bookings, connote lookups, and quotation requests, and the agent handled all of them well. Throughout the day, we made adjustments to improve workflow stability, and I explored Claude's capabilities after Christian provided us with a Claude plan. I wrapped up by logging all agent responses and compiling everything into an Excel sheet for tracking.
